title: make binary will err after make shell
body:
```
make shell
```

```
[root@052c36b618d5 skopeo]# make binary
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
user namespaces are not enabled in /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
Error: cannot re-exec process
podman run --rm -i -e TESTFLAGS="-v -check.v -test.timeout=15m" -e CI= -e SKOPEO_CONTAINER_TESTS=1 -t --security-opt label=disable -v /src/github.com/containers/skopeo:/src/github.com/containers/skopeo -w /src/github.com/containers/skopeo  make bin/skopeo  BUILDTAGS='   '
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
user namespaces are not enabled in /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
Error: cannot re-exec process
make: *** [Makefile:135: binary] Error 125
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yes; within the scope of `make shell` you are already running inside a container, so running `make binary` inside would try to create a nested container.

That’s probably not what you intended; either exit the shell (probably — `make shell` exists basically for interactive debugging of integration tests), or run `make bin/skopeo` inside the container.
